title: How to Make Moroccan Preserved Lemons with Sea Salt
description: Moroccan preserved lemons are easy to make with just two ingredients: lemons and sea salt. This salt-cured fermentation method produces lemons with a highly citrusy, floral flavor and umami depth. The whole lemon is used and they keep indefinitely when refrigerated.

ingredients.item: grams fresh lemons (about 4 to 5 small lemons)

steps: Wash all of the lemons and weigh out the salt in a bowl.
Cut the ends off the lemons, then starting at one end, cut each lemon into quarters without cutting all the way through.
Over a large bowl, fill the crevices of the lemons with copious amounts of salt until all the salt is used up.
Start packing the whole lemons into a clean, large jar, wedging them in as tightly as possible so lemon juice is released.
Once the jar is full, make sure all the lemons are submerged in lemon juice; if needed, juice the extra lemon and add the juice to the jar.
Place a lid on the jar and allow the lemons to cure at room temperature for 4 weeks.
Once opened, store in the refrigerator indefinitely; rinse off excess salt and brine before using and discard the pulp, using only the well-rinsed rind.
